REFERENCE: This reference is fabricated or severely corrupted. Multiple critical issues: (1) Author names are garbled. (2) The journal name 'J Neurogenet Ther' is fabricated - actual epcoritamab follicular lymphoma research is published in oncology journals. (3) The DOI belongs to an unrelated Science article about progeria. (4) The citation combines elements from different sources in a nonsensical way. (5) The subject matter (epcoritamab maintenance for follicular lymphoma) may be based on real research but the specific trial described (phase 3 maintenance) doesn't match real published trials as of the given date.
authors: The author names appear garbled or corrupted (e.g., '.oyal L', 'rBaun J', 'eHnze L'). The actual study on epcoritamab in follicular lymphoma has different authors (such as Hutchings et al. or similar). The names as presented do not match real authors of this study.
title: The title mentions 'epcoritamab' and 'follicular lymphoma' which relates to real research, but the specific details (subcutaneous epcoritamab maintenance therapy, phase 3 trial) may not accurately match the real study. The actual epcoritamab follicular lymphoma trial is EPCORE NHL-1 (phase 1/2), not a phase 3 maintenance study as described. Also, this is a hematology/oncology paper, not a neurology/genetics paper.
journal_name: The journal 'J Neurogenet Ther' (Journal of Neurogenetics and Therapeutics) does not match the subject matter. A study on follicular lymphoma would be published in oncology or hematology journals (e.g., Lancet Oncology, Blood, Journal of Clinical Oncology). This journal appears fabricated or incorrectly assigned.
journal_volume: The volume number format (374(6564)) resembles that of Science magazine (which uses this format), but Science is not called 'J Neurogenet Ther'. This appears to be a fabricated or incorrectly compiled citation.
journal_issue: The issue number 6564 resembles Science magazine issue numbers, not that of a journal called 'J Neurogenet Ther'. This is inconsistent with the stated journal name.
journal_page: The page range '796-838:e066952' appears inconsistent. Science articles typically don't have page ranges like this, and 'e066952' looks like an article ID but mixed with page numbers in an unusual format.
elocator: There is no separate elocator; the 'e066952' appears to be embedded in the page range, which is unusual. The DOI prefix '10.1126' corresponds to Science, not 'J Neurogenet Ther'.
publication_date: The date '2021 Oct 8' could be valid for some publications, but the combination with other incorrect elements makes it suspect. The actual epcoritamab follicular lymphoma data was published later.
doi: The DOI '10.1126/science.abh1823' corresponds to Science magazine. Checking knowledge: Science article abh1823 is 'In vivo base editing rescues Hutchinson-Gilford progeria syndrome in mice' by Koblan et al., not related to epcoritamab or follicular lymphoma at all. This DOI is clearly mismatched with the stated citation.
pmcid: No PMCID is provided in the reference.
pmid: No PMID is provided in the reference.
